subject: Re: Problem with adding multiple data sources into one M population
body:
This problem will be resolved in the next version. For now, the workaround 
is to create an empty 'matching' folder in the data source directories.

On Sunday, July 12, 2020 at 6:27:51 PM UTC+2, Linhua Tai wrote:
>
> Dear all,
> Hi, I'm trying to use M for final refinement, but having a problem with M.
>
> I used multiple data collection sessions with different gain reference and 
> exposure settings. I've done warp preprocessing on those separate 
> directories which on same hard disk. Now I need to adding multiple data 
> sources into one M population, which I created just one level up of the 
> warp preprocessing directories, the first directory for data source worked 
> fine, but for the other ones, I get the error message as: 
> 7/12/2020 10:56:10 PM:
> System.IO.DirectoryNotFoundException: Could not find a part of the path 
> 'H:\SPA_N0_1126\matching'....
> I've attached full crash history in this question.
>
> I tried several times, like save another settings file by warp, all got 
> this error message.
> Anyone got a clue how to solve this problem?
>
> Thank you very much in advance!
>
> Yours sincerely,
> Linhua Tai
>
